content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
September 1, 2016 
Grob Advice Letter No. I-16-141 is SUPERSEDED by Grob Advice Letter No. I-16-141(a) 
Michael A. Grob 
Knox, Lemmon, & Anapolsky, LLP 
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1125 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. I-16-14l(a) 
Dear Mr. Grob: 
This letter responds to your request for informal assistance on behalf of HGCPM, Inc. dba 
the Hallmark Group ("Hallmark") regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because you seek general guidance, we are providing informal assistance, 
rather than formal advice. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity 
provided by an opinion or fonnal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest, the doctrine of incompatible offices, or Section 1090. Moreover, this letter is based on the 
facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it 
renders advice. (/11 re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) 
QUESTIONS 
l. Under the Act, may Hallmark perform consulting services for the Westlands Water 
District ("Westlands") on the Lower Yolo Restoration Project (the "Lower Yolo Project,") where 
Hallmark is under contract to perform consulting services for the California Department of Water 
Resources ("DWR") on the WaterFix Project? 
2. Under the Act, may Hallmark continue to provide services for DWR after receiving 
payments from Westlands? 
3. What conflict of interest restrictions apply to Hallmark's subsequent work for DWR? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Go\'emment Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are lo the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18 l lO through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the, California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


CONCLUSIONS 
File No. 1-16-141(a) 
Page No. 2 
I. & 2. Yes. The Act does not prohibit a public official from providing services to multiple 
clients, including government agencies. 
3. Mr. Gardner may not make, participate in making or use his official position to influence 
a decision that has a. reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on Hallmark. However, under 
the government salary exception as applied to "consultants," he does not have a financial interest in 
DWR or Westlands. Therefore, he is not prohibited from making, participating in making, or 
influencing governmental decisions that have a financial effect on Westlands.2 
FACTS 
Hallmark is a California corporation with its principal business offices located in 
Sacramento, California. Hallmark provides management and consulting services for complex 
capital improvement programs. Charles Gardner Jr. is the President, Chief Executive Officer and 
sole shareholder of Hallmark. You state that Mr. Gardner files Statements of Economic Interests 
under DWR's conflict of interest code. 
DWR Contract: In 2013, Hallmark entered into a contract with DWR to perfonn work on 
the California WaterFix Project (fonnerly part of the Delta Habitat Conservation and Conveyance 
Program). The WaterFix Project involves the construction and operation of new water diversion 
facilities using two tunnels for delivering water from the Sacramento River to cities and fanns in 
the south. The WaterFix Project is funded, in part, by approximately 25 water purchasers or "water 
contractors." These water contractors are involved in the planning of those parts of the WaterFix 
Project that affect them. Westlands is one of the largest water districts in this group. The contract 
provides that Hallmark will "serve as the Program Manager ... of the initial planning, coordination 
and oversight" of the WaterFix Project. 
West/ands Contract: On June 6, 2016, Hallmark entered into a contract with Westlands to 
perfonn work on the Lower Yolo Project. Westlands owns a portion of the project site. The Lower 
Yolo Project involves restoring tidal influx to 1,100 acres of pasture land in Yolo County to protect 
native fish species and to ensure continued or enhanced flood protection. The contract provides that 
Hallmark will be responsible for managing all activities necessary to complete the Lower Yolo 
Project, support compliance with the tenns of the contract, provide overall project management, 
contract management and construction management, including management of pennitting and 
design. You have also said that Hallmark's work on the contract can be characterized as that of a 
"project manager." The contract also provides that once Hallmark's work on the Lower Yolo 
Project is completed, Westlands will enter into a contract for the sale of the site to DWR. You state 
that Westlands is affected by decisions made by DWR. Hallmark has not yet commenced work on 
the contract. 
Hallmark contemplates continuing to perfonn consulting services for DWR both during and 
after perfonning services for Westlands. 
2 A,; noted above, we do not advise on agency prohibitions such as DWR 's statement of incompatible activities 
for its officers and employees, 


ANALYSIS 
File No. I-16-141(a) 
Page No. 3 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) Holding positions with or providing services to multiple government agencies 
does not of itself create a conflict of interest under the Act. Therefore, Mr. Gardner (and his 
company) are not prohibited from performing services for Westlands or DWR. 
You have also asked us to advise Hallmark regarding potential conflicts of interest arising 
from performing services for DWR after receiving payments from Westlands. You state that 
Westlands is affected by decisions made by DWR. Because you have not identified any 
governmental decision pending before DWR, we can only provide general guidance for analyzing 
potential conflicts in connection with future DWR decisions. 
Public Official. 
The Act's conflict of interest provisions apply to all public officials including consultants of 
a state or local agency. (Sections 82048 and 87200.) We note that the term "consultant" has a very 
specific meaning under the Act, which may vary from the definition typically used for the term in 
commercial parlance. For example, a consultant for purposes of the Act is an individual and not a 
corporation. Consequently, Hallmark would not be a consultant rather Mr. Gardner may qualify as a 
consultant. 
For purposes of the Act, a "consultant" is defined in Regulation 18700.3(a) as an individual 
who, pursuant to a contract with a public agency: 
"(I) Makes a governmental decision whether to: 
"(A) Approve a rate, rule, or regulation; 
"(B) Adopt or enforce a law; 
"(C) Issue, deny, suspend, or revoke any permit, license, application, 
certificate, approval, order, or similar authorization or entitlement; 
"(D) Authorize the agency to enter into, modify, or renew a contract provided 
it is the type of contract that requires agency approval; 
"(E) Grant agency approval to a contract that requires agency approval and to 
which the agency is a party, or to the specifications for such a contract; 
"(F) Grant agency approval to a plan, design, report, study, or similar item; 
"(G) Adopt, or grant agency approval of, policies, standards, or guidelines for 
the agency, or for any subdivision thereof; or 


File No. l-16-141(a) 
Page No. 4 
"(2) Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity 
participates in making a governmental decision as defined in Regulation 18704(a) 
and (b) or performs the same or substantially all the same duties for the agency that 
would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position specified in the 
agency's Conflict of Interest Code under Section 87302. 
D1VR Contract: Mr. Gardner has served as a program manager for DWR since 2013. The 
position, "Program Manager" is a position most often held by employees. Therefore, it appears he 
serves in staff capacity. Also, Mr. Gardner files Statements of Economic Interests under DWR 's 
conflict of interest code. Based upon these facts, Mr. Gardner is a consultant of DWR and, 
therefore, is subject to the Act's conflict of interest provisions. 
West/ands Co11tract: The contract provides that Hallmark will be responsible for managing 
all activities necessary to complete the Lower Yolo Project, support compliance with the terms of 
the contract, provide overall project management, contract management, and construction 
management, including management of permitting and design. You have stated that this work can 
be characterized as that of a "project manager." These types of comprehensive management 
responsibilities for a major project are generally carried out by employees. Based upon these facts, 
it appears that Mr. Gardner would be serving Westlands in a staff capacity, making him a 
''consultant" under the Act. 
Financial Interest. 
A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's 
interests. Under Section 87103( a) and ( d), an official has an interest in any business entity in which 
the official has an investment of $2,000 or more. An official also has an interest in any source of 
income aggregating $500 or more within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
(Section 87103(c).) As the sole owner of Hallmark, Mr. Gardner has an interest in Hallmark as a 
business entity. He also has an interest in Hallmark as a source of income. 
As the I 00% owner of Hallmark, Mr. Gardner also has an interest in Hallmark's clients, 
including DWR and Westlands, as a source of income. However, the Act's definition of income 
expressly provides that income does not include salary, reimbursement of expenses and other 
specified payments received from a state, local, or federal government agency. 
{Section 82030(b)(2).) 
Regulation 18232 defines "salary" from a state, local or federal government agency to 
include fees paid to public officials as "consultants." In order for the exception to apply to payments 
Mr. Gardner receives from DWR or Westlands, he must qualify as a "consultant." Because we have 
determined that Mr. Gardner is a "consultant" of both DWR and Westlands,3 the payments he 
receives under either contracts are not "income." Accordingly, Mr. Gardner does not have a 
financial interest in either agency and would not have a conflict of interest in decisions having a 
financial effect on them. 
3 Please note that Mr. Gardner has all incumbent responsibilities as a public official with both agencies. 
including filing Form 700s. 


VJ:jgl 
File No. I-16-14la 
Page No. 5 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (9 I 6) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Valentina Joyce 
Counsel, Legal Division

I. & 2. Yes. The Act does not prohibit a public official from providing services to multiple

3. Mr. Gardner may not make, participate in making or use his official position to influence
